name: Hapa Sequential Pass Triad
summary: Routes Hapa work through speed, optionality, and production-value passes while preserving lineage and card memory.

steps.label: Compress the objective, deadline, constraints, and tolerance into the fastest acceptable solution.
explanation: For The Throat is the deadline pass. It solves the immediate problem and keeps the result inside tolerance.
The pass is fast, but it still leaves Hapa foundations: surface intent, dependency notes, registry hooks, and lineage.

steps.label: Leave future-ready protocol hooks even when the first answer is minimal.
explanation: A minimum answer should not be memoryless. It should preserve the evidence a later agent needs.
Turn Cards, Card Memory Ledgers, UI/API/CLI gaps, and node capability notes convert speed into reusable experience.

steps.label: Reopen the result while compute or time is available and compare alternatives.
explanation: Every Which Way asks what else could work now that the first answer exists.
It compares local/open-source lanes, remote/paid lanes, support-both choices, appendable improvements, and replacement candidates.

steps.label: Hand the best structural answer to a production-value pass.
explanation: With Flair assumes the structure is good enough to teach.
It turns what worked into docs, demos, cards, loops, songs, and explainers that improve understanding and retention.

steps.label: Mint or update operating cards, skill candidates, lore, songs, loops, and teaching artifacts.
explanation: The card is the reusable handle for the learned protocol.
A good card names when to play it, what it does, what can fail, and where future experience is written.

steps.label: Index pass records, card lineage, and sharpening notes for retrieval.
explanation: Second Brain makes the protocol retrievable by future humans and agents.
Feedback, creation lineage, and deployment opportunities keep the protocol from becoming passive prose.

card.description: Routes work through speed, optionality, and production value without losing lineage.
card.effect: Agents can choose the right resource stance and still produce future-useful Hapa memory.
card.risk: If the first pass skips lineage, later passes become archaeology instead of improvement.
